subject: Public Health
title: Privacy vs Public Good
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Privacy vs Public Good.

hook.text: Contact tracing saves lives—but it means tracking where sick people went and who they met.

buildup.text: During COVID-19, countries deployed digital tracking apps, raising privacy alarms worldwide.

discovery.text: South Korea published detailed movement histories of infected people—effective but invasive.

twist.text: When privacy is breached, people hide symptoms—undermining the very system meant to protect them.

climax.text: The best systems anonymize data and sunset surveillance when the crisis ends.

punchline.text: Public health needs data. People need boundaries.

quiz.question: What happens when public health surveillance is too invasive?
options: People hide symptoms, undermining disease control
